---
title: "Module 6 — SVM & Clustering"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: false
---

<!-- Thin module landing (repo convention: index.qmd is a landing, never a
     lecture deck). Decks live beside it; see CLAUDE.md. -->

The supervised arc closes with the maximum-margin classifier — support vectors,
hinge vs. logistic loss, kernels at concept level, and honest evaluation at
38.8% prevalence. Then the unsupervised turn: delete the labels and let k-means
find structure in the features alone — assign/update, distortion $\mathcal{L}$,
local optima, choosing $K$ — ending with the course's honesty rule: clusters
enrich risk, but **clusters are not diagnoses**.

| Deck | Link |
|------|------|
| Lecture A — Maximum-Margin Classification | [m6a-svm](m6a-svm.html) |
| Lecture B — k-means & the Unsupervised Turn | [m6b-kmeans-clustering](m6b-kmeans-clustering.html) |

Companion activity: [week05 k-means explorer](https://gchism94.github.io/info521-activities-2026/week05-kmeans/)
(embedded live in Lecture B — the slug is week-named; modules ≠ weeks).

[← All slide decks](../../index.html)
